name: better-zsh
description: Improved zsh shellscript code editing experience

betterZsh.zshPath.markdownDescription: Path to the zsh binary. Leave empty to use `zsh` from PATH. Set to `off` to never invoke any zsh binary.

languageModelTools.name: zsh_docs
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Look up the docs for a raw zsh token in the bundled static zsh-5.9 reference.

Returns one match per category that resolves the input, each with the rendered markdown body.

Categories searched (in classify-walk order):

  - 'complex_command'  (complex command)
  - 'reserved_word'  (reserved word)
  - 'precmd'  (precommand modifier)
  - 'builtin'  (builtin)
  - 'cond_op'  (conditional operator)
  - 'special_function'  (special function)
  - 'shell_param'  (shell parameter)
  - 'process_subst'  (process substitution)
  - 'param_expn'  (parameter-expansion form)
  - 'param_flag'  (parameter-expansion flag)
  - 'subscript_flag'  (subscript flag)
  - 'glob_flag'  (glob flag)
  - 'glob_qualifier'  (glob qualifier)
  - 'glob_op'  (glob operator)
  - 'history'  (history designator)
  - 'prompt_escape'  (prompt escape)
  - 'job_spec'  (job spec)
  - 'zle_widget'  (ZLE widget)
  - 'keymap'  (ZLE keymap)
  - 'arith_op'  (arithmetic operator)
  - 'option'  (option)
  - 'redir'  (redirection)

Set `category` to constrain the search to one category; otherwise every category is tried and the response may carry more than one match. Some inputs name elements in more than one category (e.g. `for`, `[[`, `function`, `nocorrect`); without `category` those return multiple matches.

Each match is `{ category, id, display, markdown }`. Option matches additionally carry `negated: true|false` so agents can distinguish `setopt AUTO_CD` from `setopt NO_AUTO_CD` (handles the NOTIFY / NO_NOTIFY edge case).

Resolution is corpus-aware: case-insensitive option matching, underscore stripping, redirection group-op + tail decomposition, history event-designators, and the option `NO_*` negation convention. Canonical record ids (e.g. `autocd`) round-trip exactly.

Returns `{ matches: [], matchesReturned: 0, matchesTotal: 0 }` when nothing resolves. `matchesReturned` and `matchesTotal` are emitted for envelope uniformity with `zsh_search` / `zsh_list`; `docs` never truncates, so they always equal `matches.length`.

No shell execution, no environment access.

raw.description: The raw token as it might appear in zsh source — e.g. "AUTO_CD", "echo", "[[", "<<<", "!$", "%1", "NO_NOTIFY", or a canonical id from a prior `zsh_search` such as "autocd". Case and underscores are normalized per category.

category.description:
Optional: constrain the lookup to one category. When omitted, every category is tried and the response may carry more than one match. Unknown values yield an empty match set.

Valid values:

  - 'complex_command'  (complex command)
  - 'reserved_word'  (reserved word)
  - 'precmd'  (precommand modifier)
  - 'builtin'  (builtin)
  - 'cond_op'  (conditional operator)
  - 'special_function'  (special function)
  - 'shell_param'  (shell parameter)
  - 'process_subst'  (process substitution)
  - 'param_expn'  (parameter-expansion form)
  - 'param_flag'  (parameter-expansion flag)
  - 'subscript_flag'  (subscript flag)
  - 'glob_flag'  (glob flag)
  - 'glob_qualifier'  (glob qualifier)
  - 'glob_op'  (glob operator)
  - 'history'  (history designator)
  - 'prompt_escape'  (prompt escape)
  - 'job_spec'  (job spec)
  - 'zle_widget'  (ZLE widget)
  - 'keymap'  (ZLE keymap)
  - 'arith_op'  (arithmetic operator)
  - 'option'  (option)
  - 'redir'  (redirection)

languageModelTools.name: zsh_search
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Search the bundled static zsh-5.9 reference. Fuzzy-matches the query against record ids and display headings across every category (or one category if `category` is set).

Ranking: exact id/display > prefix > fuzzy score.

Results carry `{ category, id, display, subKind?, score? }` but NOT the rendered markdown body — follow up with `zsh_docs` for the full doc. `subKind` is surfaced when the category has a meaningful sub-facet (e.g. history `kind`, glob_op `kind`, reserved_word `pos`).

`limit` caps response size (default 20, hard max 1128 = entire corpus). `limit=0` returns metadata only (`matches: []`); the response always carries `matchesReturned` (== `matches.length`) and `matchesTotal` (pre-truncation total), so `matchesReturned < matchesTotal` signals truncation — raise `limit` or narrow `category`/`query` to see the rest.

To enumerate without a query, use `zsh_list`.

Valid `category` values:

  - 'option'
  - 'cond_op'
  - 'builtin'
  - 'precmd'
  - 'shell_param'
  - 'complex_command'
  - 'reserved_word'
  - 'redir'
  - 'process_subst'
  - 'param_expn'
  - 'subscript_flag'
  - 'param_flag'
  - 'history'
  - 'glob_op'
  - 'glob_flag'
  - 'glob_qualifier'
  - 'prompt_escape'
  - 'zle_widget'
  - 'keymap'
  - 'job_spec'
  - 'arith_op'
  - 'special_function'

No shell execution, no environment access.

query.description:
Fuzzy search string matched against ids and display headings. Empty/whitespace returns an empty match set — use `zsh_list` to enumerate.

Ranking: exact id/display > prefix > fuzzy score.

category.description:
Optional filter to a single doc category. Unknown categories yield an empty match set.

Valid values:

  - 'option'
  - 'cond_op'
  - 'builtin'
  - 'precmd'
  - 'shell_param'
  - 'complex_command'
  - 'reserved_word'
  - 'redir'
  - 'process_subst'
  - 'param_expn'
  - 'subscript_flag'
  - 'param_flag'
  - 'history'
  - 'glob_op'
  - 'glob_flag'
  - 'glob_qualifier'
  - 'prompt_escape'
  - 'zle_widget'
  - 'keymap'
  - 'job_spec'
  - 'arith_op'
  - 'special_function'

limit.description:
Maximum matches to return. Default 20, hard max 1128 (entire corpus). `limit=0` returns metadata only.

The response carries `matchesReturned` (== `matches.length`) and `matchesTotal` (pre-truncation total); `matchesReturned < matchesTotal` signals truncation — raise `limit` or narrow `category`/`query`.

languageModelTools.name: zsh_list
languageModelTools.modelDescription:
Enumerate records from the bundled static zsh reference. Identifiers only — pair with `zsh_docs` for the rendered markdown body.

Listing is corpus-iteration order; default category order when `category` is omitted, or the single category's iteration order when set.

Each match is `{ category, id, display, subKind? }`. `subKind` is surfaced when the category has a meaningful sub-facet (e.g. history `kind`, glob_op `kind`, reserved_word `pos`).

`limit` caps response size (default 20, hard max 1128 = entire corpus). `limit=0` returns metadata only (`matches: []`); the response always carries `matchesReturned` (== `matches.length`) and `matchesTotal` (pre-truncation total), so `matchesReturned < matchesTotal` signals truncation — raise `limit` or narrow `category` to see the rest.

Valid `category` values:

  - 'option'
  - 'cond_op'
  - 'builtin'
  - 'precmd'
  - 'shell_param'
  - 'complex_command'
  - 'reserved_word'
  - 'redir'
  - 'process_subst'
  - 'param_expn'
  - 'subscript_flag'
  - 'param_flag'
  - 'history'
  - 'glob_op'
  - 'glob_flag'
  - 'glob_qualifier'
  - 'prompt_escape'
  - 'zle_widget'
  - 'keymap'
  - 'job_spec'
  - 'arith_op'
  - 'special_function'

Unknown `category` yields an empty match set.

No shell execution, no environment access.

category.description:
Optional filter to a single doc category. Unknown categories yield an empty match set.

Valid values:

  - 'option'
  - 'cond_op'
  - 'builtin'
  - 'precmd'
  - 'shell_param'
  - 'complex_command'
  - 'reserved_word'
  - 'redir'
  - 'process_subst'
  - 'param_expn'
  - 'subscript_flag'
  - 'param_flag'
  - 'history'
  - 'glob_op'
  - 'glob_flag'
  - 'glob_qualifier'
  - 'prompt_escape'
  - 'zle_widget'
  - 'keymap'
  - 'job_spec'
  - 'arith_op'
  - 'special_function'

limit.description:
Maximum matches to return. Default 20, hard max 1128 (entire corpus). `limit=0` returns metadata only.

The response carries `matchesReturned` (== `matches.length`) and `matchesTotal` (pre-truncation total); `matchesReturned < matchesTotal` signals truncation — raise `limit` or narrow `category`.

scripts.format: biome check --error-on-warnings --write src build.ts tsconfig.json tsconfig.vscode-test.json vitest.config.ts package.json .vscodeignore .vscode-test.mjs scripts
scripts.lint: biome check --error-on-warnings src build.ts tsconfig.json tsconfig.vscode-test.json vitest.config.ts package.json .vscodeignore .vscode-test.mjs scripts
scripts.testINTERACTIVE:electron-bundled: pnpm build && scripts/package-vsix ./better-zsh.vsix && tsc -p tsconfig.vscode-test.json && scripts/testINTERACTIVE-bundled
scripts.vsix: pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && scripts/package-vsix ./better-zsh.vsix
scripts.test:smoke: pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && scripts/package-vsix /tmp/better-zsh-smoke.vsix && rm /tmp/better-zsh-smoke.vsix && echo 'smoke: OK'
scripts.vscode:publish:dry:
pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && pnpm exec vsce ls --no-dependencies && echo '---
(DRY-RUN; ran vsce ls above.)'
scripts.vscode:publish: pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && pnpm exec vsce publish --no-dependencies
scripts.ovsx:publish:dry:
pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && pnpm exec vsce ls --no-dependencies && echo '---
(DRY-RUN for open-vsx; ran vsce ls above.)'
scripts.ovsx:publish: pnpm build && export BZ_SKIP_UPSTREAM=1 && pnpm check && pnpm test && npx -y ovsx -p $OPEN_VSX_TOKEN publish --no-dependencies
